title: CulinaryOS
tagline: The operating system for the modern kitchen.
description: CulinaryOS is a modular, multi-service platform that unifies every layer of restaurant operations — from recipe management and prep ticketing to point-of-sale and analytics — into one coherent system.
story: Built after years of watching kitchen staff juggle five different apps during a dinner rush. Every station had its own screen, its own tool, its own failure point. CulinaryOS started as a question: what if the whole kitchen ran on one OS?
forWho: Restaurant owners, head chefs, and kitchen managers who need operations software built by someone who has actually worked the line.

detailedSpecs.architecture: Microservices with a shared PostgreSQL core. Each module (RecipeOS, KDS, POS) runs as an independent service that communicates via a lightweight internal event bus.
detailedSpecs.dataFlow: Order placed in POS → event emitted → KDS receives ticket → recipe data pulled from RecipeOS → completion event logged for analytics.
keyPatterns: Shared DB schema with service-scoped tables
